behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed; unchanged files are excluded.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: PR changed-file list must be consumed and correctly filtered before analysis. Hypothetical elsewhere: GitHub API outages, runner-level filesystem issues. MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (change detector -> analyzer target set), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess to reproduce filtering bug. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text only requires 'files changed in the pull request are analyzed' and does not require validating real network/runner behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire PR-diff parsing module to analysis scheduler with fixture PR metadata and repo snapshot; assert only changed paths are passed to scanner and unchanged paths are never enqueued.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: findings from analysis must be transformed into grouped comment content and posted payload format. Hypothetical elsewhere: GitHub API auth/rate-limit behavior. MOCK-MISS: grouping/formatting bugs often appear at boundary between findings schema and comment renderer. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process fake PR-comment client; real network not required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text focuses on grouping in comment content, not delivery reliability under real GitHub infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run analyzer-output -> comment-builder -> comment-publisher flow with a fake GitHub client; assert generated posted body contains distinct error/warning/info sections with correct finding membership.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: runtime/performance under CI timeout budget in GitHub Actions context. Hypothetical elsewhere: external API latency spikes not part of this specific check unless used by lint step. REAL-INFRA: timeout behavior is tied to runner/process execution characteristics; in-process mocks can hide true runtime costs. MOCK-HIDE: mocked execution would likely pass and miss real wall-clock regressions. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly states 'must complete within the Actions timeout' (real CI execution boundary).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step in a real CI-like/system test (same invocation path as GitHub Action) against a 200-file PR fixture and assert end-to-end wall-clock completion is under 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, remaining files are still analyzed and results are posted.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: crash of 'linter process' plus continuation and final posting. Hypothetical elsewhere: distributed retries, network partitions. REAL-INFRA: requires real subprocess crash semantics (exit code/signal/partial output) to catch failure handling. MOCK-HIDE: replacing crash with a normal mocked error can miss supervisor/recovery defects. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text explicitly requires handling a 'linter process crashes' condition, which is subprocess-level.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with the real linter executable on a corpus including one malformed file that triggers a process crash; verify pipeline continues scanning remaining files and emits final PR-comment payload with surviving findings plus crash notice.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in output.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: dedup logic over finding tuples (file, line, rule overlap, message/severity keying) in output set. Hypothetical elsewhere: comment posting failures, subprocess behavior. MOCK-MISS: specific failure is algorithmic/data-structure logic in one boundary; no required module wiring beyond function/object contract. REAL-INFRA: none required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agent pipeline: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration rejected because behavior text is limited to deduplication correctness and does not require cross-module infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a dedup function/object a synthetic findings list containing overlapping-rule duplicates on identical file+line and assert output keeps one canonical entry per dedup key while preserving distinct non-duplicates.
